channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: United Arab Emirates announces it's leaving OPEC
item.description: The UAE says it will leave OPEC, amid tensions with Saudi Arabia and the chaos of the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: The UAE says it will leave OPEC, amid tensions with Saudi Arabia and the chaos of the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: DOJ indicts former FBI director James Comey for a second time
item.description: The Justice Department has indicted former FBI Director James Comey, months after its first case against him collapsed. President Trump has been demanding retribution against his critics.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department has indicted former FBI Director James Comey, months after its first case against him collapsed. President Trump has been demanding retribution against his critics.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: What's in the second indictment of former FBI director James Comey?
item.description: What is the substance of the new DOJ charges against former FBI Director James Comey? NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Benjamin Wittes, editor in chief of Lawfare.
item.content:encoded: What is the substance of the new DOJ charges against former FBI Director James Comey? NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Benjamin Wittes, editor in chief of Lawfare.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Purdue Pharma sentenced in criminal opioid case while company leaders avoid charges
item.description: Purdue Pharma will pay the DOJ $225 million in a criminal settlement and members of the Sackler family who own the Oxycontin-maker also contribute billions of dollars to a bankruptcy deal, but the private drug firm's leaders will avoid prison time.
item.content:encoded: Purdue Pharma will pay the DOJ $225 million in a criminal settlement and members of the Sackler family who own the Oxycontin-maker also contribute billions of dollars to a bankruptcy deal, but the private drug firm's leaders will avoid prison time.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Texas' hemp industry fights new regulations that have upended the legal hemp market
item.description: One of the country's largest legal cannabis markets is fighting for its life. Texas' hemp industry is challenging fee increases of up to 4,000%, which effectively ban cannabis' most popular form.
item.content:encoded: One of the country's largest legal cannabis markets is fighting for its life. Texas' hemp industry is challenging fee increases of up to 4,000%, which effectively ban cannabis' most popular form.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Bernier

item.title: Supreme Court weighs Trump's effort to end temporary protected status for Haitians, Syrians
item.description: At issue is the TPS program, which permits eligible individuals to live and work in the United States if they cannot return to their home countries because of "extraordinary or temporary conditions."
item.content:encoded:
At issue is the TPS program, which permits eligible individuals to live and work in the United States if they cannot return to their home countries because of "extraordinary or temporary conditions."

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: In the midst of an energy crisis, countries make plans to ditch oil, gas and coal
item.description: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.
item.content:encoded:
Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.

(Image credit: Raul Arboleda)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: DOJ indicts former FBI Director James Comey for second time, King Charles argues for stronger U.K.-U.S. relations, SCOTUS weighs Trump's effort to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitians, Syrians.
item.content:encoded: DOJ indicts former FBI Director James Comey for second time, King Charles argues for stronger U.K.-U.S. relations, SCOTUS weighs Trump's effort to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitians, Syrians.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: What 'The Devil Wears Prada' got right about the fashion industry
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with fashion critic Robin Givhan about the fashion industry's opinion of "The Devil Wears Prada" movie and what it's hoping to see -- and not see -- in the sequel.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with fashion critic Robin Givhan about the fashion industry's opinion of "The Devil Wears Prada" movie and what it's hoping to see -- and not see -- in the sequel.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Party infighting and revolts continue to complicate House Speaker Mike Johnson's job
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is trying to pass several major pieces of legislation, but party infighting has complicated his job -- and by extension the GOP's agenda.
item.content:encoded: House Speaker Mike Johnson is trying to pass several major pieces of legislation, but party infighting has complicated his job -- and by extension the GOP's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales
